$schema_note: Festival roster for /scripts/festivals-prompt.md. Two-class architecture: discovery (aggregators used as candidate-list scaffolding, never cited as source_url) and primary (festival's own official site, citation target). Read by Claude headless harvest weekly. Quantity-is-quality: agent should crawl every discovery source for candidates, then verify and extract from primary sites.
$architecture: Two-pass scrape-within-scrape. Pass 1: read every discovery source, build candidate list of festival names + provisional date windows + official URLs. Discard discovery entries after this pass. Pass 2: for each candidate, fetch its primary official site, confirm date, extract program, build record with primary URL as source_url. Drop candidates the primary site does not confirm.
$audit_rule: No record's source_url may match a discovery hostname. merge_festivals.py enforces this as defense-in-depth.

discovery_aggregators.name: To Do Canada — Toronto festivals
discovery_aggregators.notes: Indexes ~50 festivals across the Toronto calendar. Reliable for big-anchor coverage.

discovery_aggregators.name: Streets Of Toronto — culture index
discovery_aggregators.notes: Monthly '76 best things' format gives broad small-event coverage.

discovery_aggregators.name: BlogTO events
discovery_aggregators.notes: Local culture indexing. Pagination is heavy; budget tokens accordingly.

discovery_aggregators.name: Ontario Festivals — GTA section
discovery_aggregators.notes: Suburban festival coverage (Vaughan, Markham, Mississauga, Brampton, Oakville).

discovery_aggregators.name: Music Festival Wizard — Toronto
discovery_aggregators.notes: Music-festival specialist aggregator. Cross-check date claims against primary sites; user flagged false-positive risk.

discovery_aggregators.name: Canadian Train Vacations — Toronto summer festivals
discovery_aggregators.notes: Tourism-blog summary. Big-anchor coverage. Travel-industry framing.

discovery_aggregators.name: Now Toronto
discovery_aggregators.notes: Cultural weekly. Festival coverage runs adjacent to event-pick coverage.

discovery_aggregators.name: Destination Toronto official tourism site
discovery_aggregators.notes: Tourism authority. Heavy on Tier-1 anchors. Low coverage of fringe events.

discovery_aggregators.name: City of Toronto events calendar
discovery_aggregators.notes: Municipal listing. Doors Open, Nuit Blanche, civic festivals.

primary_sources.name: CONTACT Photography Festival
primary_sources.notes: May 1-31 each year. Hundreds of sub-events at galleries city-wide. Agent should capture parent festival + Featured Exhibitions as separate records, skip the long tail.

primary_sources.name: imagineNATIVE Film + Media Arts Festival
primary_sources.notes: Indigenous-led film festival. June each year. Films with director Q&A overlap with screening-type criteria.

primary_sources.name: Toronto Jazz Festival
primary_sources.notes: Late June. ~500,000 attendees. Multi-venue. Capture parent festival + key named-artist concerts.

primary_sources.name: Pride Toronto
primary_sources.notes: Late June. Church-Wellesley. Capture parent festival + major Pride Month events including Trans March, Dyke March, Pride Parade, Streetfair.

primary_sources.name: Luminato Festival
primary_sources.notes: Mid-June typically. Interdisciplinary arts. Capture parent + headline performances.

primary_sources.name: NXNE (North by Northeast)
primary_sources.notes: Mid-June. Music + interactive + film. Multi-day, multi-venue.

primary_sources.name: Toronto Comic Arts Festival
primary_sources.notes: Mid-May to mid-June typically. Toronto Reference Library. Independent comics.

primary_sources.name: TIFF (Toronto International Film Festival)
primary_sources.notes: September. Distinct from year-round TIFF Lightbox programming. Heavy director-Q&A density.

primary_sources.name: Hot Docs Festival
primary_sources.notes: Late April through early May. Documentary festival. Distinct from year-round Hot Docs Ted Rogers Cinema programming.

primary_sources.name: Doors Open Toronto
primary_sources.notes: Last weekend of May. Free public access to buildings normally closed. Civic-architectural ritual.

primary_sources.name: Toronto International Festival of Authors
primary_sources.notes: Late October. Harbourfront. Literary readings, panels, talks. Matches both seminar-orthodox and festival-of-form.

primary_sources.name: Toronto Caribbean Carnival
primary_sources.notes: Late July through early August. Caribbean-diasporic festival of form. Multi-week program.

primary_sources.name: SING! The Toronto Vocal Arts Festival
primary_sources.notes: Late May through early June. A cappella and vocal arts.

primary_sources.name: Jewish Music Week
primary_sources.notes: Late May. Diasporic-cultural festival of form.

primary_sources.name: Nuit Blanche Toronto
primary_sources.notes: Early October. City-wide overnight contemporary art.

primary_sources.name: Harbourfront Centre Summer Music
primary_sources.notes: Multi-month summer programming. Many cultural-diasporic anchors (Latin, Caribbean, Afro, etc.).

primary_sources.name: Toronto Fringe Festival
primary_sources.notes: Early July. Independent theatre. Lottery-selected programming.

primary_sources.name: SummerWorks Performance Festival
primary_sources.notes: Early August. Curated contemporary performance. Higher-art-stake counterpart to Fringe.

primary_sources.name: Toronto Buskerfest
primary_sources.notes: Late August. Street performance. Yonge-Dundas Square typical venue.

primary_sources.name: Toronto Black Film Festival
primary_sources.notes: February. Black-cinema festival. Director Q&A standard.

primary_sources.name: Toronto Reel Asian Film Festival
primary_sources.notes: November. Asian-diasporic cinema.

primary_sources.name: Inside Out 2SLGBTQ+ Film Festival
primary_sources.notes: Late May to early June. Queer cinema. Strong director-attendance density.

primary_sources.name: Polish Festival Mississauga
primary_sources.notes: Late summer typically. Diasporic-cultural reproduction. Suburban GTA.

primary_sources.name: Carassauga (Mississauga Festival of Cultures)
primary_sources.notes: May. Multi-pavilion diasporic-cultural showcase.

primary_sources.name: Salsa on St. Clair
primary_sources.notes: Mid-July. Latin-diasporic street festival.

primary_sources.name: Afrofest
primary_sources.notes: Early July. Woodbine Park. African-diasporic music festival.

primary_sources.name: Tirgan Festival
primary_sources.notes: Late July. Iranian-diasporic festival of form. Harbourfront.

primary_sources.name: Chaharshanbe Suri (Iranian fire-jumping festival)
primary_sources.notes: Last Wednesday before Persian New Year (March). Mel Lastman Square typical. Officially-supported festival of form. May lack dedicated primary site; the City of Toronto events page is the closest primary source.

primary_sources.name: Krinos Taste of the Danforth
primary_sources.notes: Early August. Greek-diasporic festival. Largest street festival in Canada.

primary_sources.name: VELD Music Festival
primary_sources.notes: Early August. Downsview Park. EDM. Borderline cultural-form criterion; included for completeness.

primary_sources.name: Festival of South Asia
primary_sources.notes: Late July. Gerrard India Bazaar. Diasporic festival.
